title: Tennis Stars Alcaraz and Djokovic Pull Out of Madrid Open Due to Injuries
summary:
Bad news for tennis fans! Two huge stars — Carlos Alcaraz and Novak Djokovic — have pulled out of the Madrid Open next week. Both are injured.

Alcaraz is ranked number two in the world. He's 22 years old and has already won seven Grand Slam titles (the biggest tennis tournaments). He has a wrist problem that also made him miss the Barcelona Open this week. Alcaraz is from Spain, so Madrid is like a home tournament for him. He said on Instagram that missing it was 'incredibly difficult' and that it hurt not to play in front of his fans for the second year in a row.

Djokovic is 38 and has won 24 Grand Slam titles — more than any man in history. He hasn't played since March, when he lost at Indian Wells. He has a shoulder injury and skipped tournaments in Miami and Monte Carlo. He said on Instagram he's continuing his recovery to be back soon.

Both players are in a race to get fit for the French Open, which starts on May 18. Alcaraz has won the French Open the last two years and wants to win it a third time. Also, British player Jack Draper hurt his knee in Barcelona and is also trying to be ready for the French Open.
why_it_matters: Even top athletes get injured and have to miss events they love, just like you might miss a game or activity because you're hurt — it's frustrating but part of staying healthy.

A tennis star from Serbia, with 24 Grand Slam titles, more than any man

How many Grand Slam titles has Djokovic won?
Which tournament is Alcaraz trying to win for the third time in a row?

Which other British player is also injured and trying to be ready for the French Open?

background_read: The French Open is one of the four biggest tennis tournaments in the world, played on clay courts.
Injuries are common in sports, and many athletes have to skip tournaments to recover.
Carlos Alcaraz is from Spain, so playing in Madrid is special because he has lots of fans there.
Article_Structure: WHO: Carlos Alcaraz (Spain, ranked #2) and Novak Djokovic (Serbia, 24 Grand Slams)
WHAT: Both players have pulled out of the Madrid Open due to injuries
WHY: They are injured and need to recover to be ready for the French Open starting May 18

perspectives.description: Disappointed because they won't get to see two superstars play in Madrid.
Frustrated and sad, but focused on recovery for the bigger tournament ahead.
May see an opportunity to win matches they might not have won otherwise.
